$description: Semantic foundations — non-colour intent tokens that live at the semantic tier because they encode a design decision rather than a raw value. Theme- and brand-agnostic; consumers of any tier may bind to these. Folded together in one file because each group is small and they share the same tier.

rule.$description: Stroke/divider/accent-rule thicknesses. Borders use `hair`; focus outlines use `thin`; tone-coloured accent stripes (e.g. sprout-card top rule) use `regular`; heavy accents `thick`.

touch-target.$description: Minimum interactive size for tappable affordances (steppers, drag handles, segmented controls, mobile-bar buttons). `regular` (48px) is the WCAG 2.5.5 AAA finger-friendly default; `compact` (40px) for dense desktop UIs; `small` (32px) for icon-only secondary actions where the affordance is supported by surrounding context.

tone-mix.$description: Mix ratios used by `color-mix(in oklch, var(--tone) <ratio>, var(--surface-raised))` to derive tinted backgrounds from any tone (property tone, status, action). Decoupled from the tone source so the same ratio applies whether the consumer is a card, a chip, a hover row, or a chart tooltip. Authored as 0–1 numbers so CSS can format as percent with `calc()` if needed; the standard CSS form `var(--colour-tone-mix-tint-ratio)` resolves to `0.12` and is consumed as the percent in `color-mix`.

tint.$description: Default tone tint — icon pill backings, edit-chip backings, soft hover surfaces.

tint-strong.$description: Stronger tint — hover/active states over a tinted surface, selected rows.

motion.$description: Semantic motion intents that alias the raw timing primitives. UI consumers bind to these names; the primitives can shift without rewriting components.

fast.$description: Small state changes, button presses, focus rings.

base.$description: Default UI transitions — Seed→Sprout expand, default fades.

easing.$description: Standard easing curves at semantic tier. Authored here (not as aliases) because the names are intent-bearing — primitives stayed numeric/descriptive.

decelerate.$description: Soft ease-out for entering UI, hover lifts.

swift.$description: Quick reactive + long fade. Sharp initial velocity (≈8.5), gentle landing (≈0). Use for hover lifts where the card should respond instantly then settle softly. Sharper attack than `decelerate`, less springy than `emphasized`.
